input.tenant_message: I've noticed some hairline cracks appearing along the ceiling where it meets the walls in two rooms. They're thin and not very wide, but there are quite a few of them in a row. Everything else in the unit seems fine and it doesn't look like anything is leaking. Is this something I should be worried about?

ai_output.recommended_action: Hairline cracks at wall-ceiling joints are commonly caused by normal seasonal settling and thermal expansion — monitor for widening or new cracks over the next 30-60 days. Schedule a routine inspection at the next available opportunity to confirm no underlying movement. Cosmetic repair with joint compound and paint can be addressed during the next scheduled maintenance visit.
